name: Tinderbox Docs Portal
description: Developer documentation portal for the Tinderbox CLI. Compares a text-first reading experience against a playground-first try-it-now layout, across single-column and three-pane layouts.

choices.name: home
choices.description: Landing — what Tinderbox is and why.

axes.name: variant
axes.description: Content stance. Only meaningful on home and quickstart; the API reference uses 'none'.

choices.name: none
choices.description: No variant treatment; used for pages where the stance is fixed.

choices.name: text-first
choices.description: Long-form prose lead. Code samples inline. Designed for readers who learn by reading.

choices.name: playground-first
choices.description: Editable code sample on top, explanation below. Designed for readers who learn by tinkering.

choices.name: paper
choices.description: Warm off-white background with a serif body face. Reading-optimized.

choices.name: console
choices.description: Dark terminal-adjacent palette with a mono body face for code-heavy pages.

choices.name: single-column
choices.description: Centered prose, no sidebars. Optimal line length, minimal chrome.

choices.name: three-pane
choices.description: Left TOC, center content, right on-page outline. Reference-style.

pages.description: Same landing copy reframed as 'try it now' with editable command blocks.
Reading mode in console palette — useful pressure test for the serif/mono swap.
Reference content forced into the reading layout — included to show why it doesn't work.
